description: Make procurement lead-finding robust and cheap by adding an HTTP+Floki fetch tier and resolving how each portal class is reached. Empirically established 2026-06-22: (1) Agency sites (OCWD, server-rendered WP) are fully reachable via plain Req+Floki -- 53 complete rows vs the browser's 20 lossy ones, no agent-browser needed. (2) OpenGov = Cloudflare 403 even with browser headers. (3) PlanetBids = AWS WAF (passes with Chrome UA) but Ember SPA + papi OAuth API. So: HTTP-first for agency sites; browser (or official API) stays essential for SaaS platforms. No crawling framework (Crawly/kreuzcrawl) -- Req+Floki+Oban suffices and frameworks don't beat the WAF/SPA wall. Browser env blocker already fixed (commit 2d3ccc6).
title: Lead scanning: HTTP-first scraping tier + SaaS platform coverage
